label: plain_text
text:
The correct use of reasoning is at the core of mathematics, especially i
in constructing
proofs. In Class IX, you were introduced to the idea of proofs, and you actually proved
statements, especially in
geometry. Recall that a proof is made up of several
many
mathematical statements, each of which is logically deduced from a previous statement
in the proof, or from a theorem
proved earlier, or an axiom, or the hypotheses. The
main tool,
 we use in constructing a proof, is the
e process of deductive reasoning.
